title_en: The Cure: Repentance, Love of Allah, and the Weapons Against Sin
title_id: Obatnya: Taubat, Cinta kepada Allah, dan Senjata Melawan Dosa
content_en:
If the disease analyzed in Ad-Da' wad-Dawa' is sin and its effects on the heart, the cure that Ibn al-Qayyim prescribes is multidimensional — a combination of repentance, love, knowledge, and specific spiritual practices that together heal the damaged heart and restore its orientation toward Allah.

Repentance (tawbah) is the gateway to all healing, and Ibn al-Qayyim's treatment of it is among the most comprehensive in Islamic literature. He defines sincere repentance as having three necessary conditions: genuine remorse for the sin, cessation of the sin at the moment of repentance, and a firm determination never to return to it. If the sin involved another person's rights, making restitution is a fourth necessary condition. He also addresses the question of whether repentance is accepted after multiple relapses — answering emphatically that Allah's door of repentance is never closed as long as the soul remains in the body, and that no sin is too great for divine forgiveness if the repentance is sincere.

Beyond repentance, Ibn al-Qayyim argues that the most powerful weapon against sin is genuine love of Allah. When the heart truly loves Allah — experiences His names and attributes as realities, feels connected to Him in prayer and dhikr, and finds genuine sweetness in worship — the attractions of sin lose their power. The person who is drunk on love of the Creator is not easily seduced by the passing pleasures of disobedience. This is why Ibn al-Qayyim devotes extensive sections of the book to cultivating love of Allah through reflection on His attributes, his generosity, and the gifts He has given the believer.

The book also presents practical weapons against specific sins. For sins of the eyes, it recommends lowering the gaze and filling the heart with reflection on the hereafter. For sins of the tongue, it recommends silence, remembrance of Allah, and recitation of Quran. For sins of the stomach, it recommends controlling appetite and remembering the accountability on the Day of Judgment. For sins driven by the love of status, it recommends reflection on the insignificance of worldly reputation and the permanence of Allah's judgment. Each prescription is tailored to the specific disease it addresses.
content_id:
Jika penyakit yang dianalisis dalam Ad-Da' wad-Dawa' adalah dosa dan dampaknya terhadap hati, maka obat yang diresepkan Ibn al-Qayyim bersifat multidimensi — perpaduan antara taubat, cinta, ilmu, dan praktik-praktik spiritual tertentu yang secara bersama-sama menyembuhkan hati yang rusak dan memulihkan orientasinya kepada Allah.

Taubat adalah pintu menuju segala penyembuhan, dan pembahasan Ibn al-Qayyim tentangnya termasuk yang paling komprehensif dalam literatur Islam. Beliau mendefinisikan taubat yang tulus sebagai memiliki tiga syarat yang diperlukan: penyesalan yang tulus atas dosa tersebut, penghentian dosa pada saat bertaubat, dan tekad kuat untuk tidak pernah kembali kepadanya. Jika dosa tersebut melibatkan hak orang lain, maka mengganti kerugian adalah syarat keempat yang diperlukan. Beliau juga membahas pertanyaan apakah taubat diterima setelah beberapa kali kambuh — dengan menjawab secara tegas bahwa pintu taubat Allah tidak pernah tertutup selama jiwa masih berada di dalam tubuh, dan bahwa tidak ada dosa yang terlalu besar untuk diampuni Allah jika taubatnya tulus.

Di luar taubat, Ibn al-Qayyim berargumen bahwa senjata paling ampuh melawan dosa adalah cinta yang tulus kepada Allah. Ketika hati benar-benar mencintai Allah — merasakan nama-nama dan sifat-sifat-Nya sebagai realitas, merasa terhubung kepada-Nya dalam salat dan dzikir, dan menemukan manisnya ibadah yang sejati — daya tarik dosa pun kehilangan kekuatannya. Orang yang mabuk cinta kepada Sang Pencipta tidak mudah tergoda oleh kesenangan sementara dari kemaksiatan. Inilah mengapa Ibn al-Qayyim mendedikasikan bagian-bagian yang luas dalam kitab ini untuk menumbuhkan kecintaan kepada Allah melalui perenungan atas sifat-sifat-Nya, kedermawanan-Nya, dan karunia-karunia yang telah Ia berikan kepada orang yang beriman.

Kitab ini juga menyajikan senjata-senjata praktis melawan dosa-dosa tertentu. Untuk dosa mata, beliau merekomendasikan menundukkan pandangan dan memenuhi hati dengan perenungan tentang akhirat. Untuk dosa lisan, beliau merekomendasikan diam, dzikrullah, dan tilawah Al-Quran. Untuk dosa perut, beliau merekomendasikan pengendalian nafsu makan dan mengingat pertanggungjawaban di Hari Kiamat. Untuk dosa yang didorong oleh cinta kedudukan, beliau merekomendasikan perenungan tentang rendahnya reputasi duniawi dan keabadian hukum Allah. Setiap resep disesuaikan dengan penyakit spesifik yang ditanganinya.
content_ar: إذا the المرض analyzed in Ad-Da' wad-Dawa' is الذنب and its effects on the القلب, the الدواء that ابن القيم prescribes is multidimensional — a combination of التوبة, المحبة, العلم, and specific spiritual practices that together heal the damaged القلب and restore its orientation toward الله. التوبة (التوبة) is the gateway to all الشفاء, and ابن القيم's treatment of it is among the most comprehensive in Islamic literature. He defines sincere التوبة as having three necessary conditions: genuine remorse for the الذنب, cessation of the الذنب at the moment of التوبة, and a firm determination never to return to it. إذا the الذنب involved another person's الحقوق, making restitution is a رابعاً necessary condition. He أيضاً addresses the question of whether التوبة is accepted بعد multiple relapses — answering emphatically that الله's door of التوبة is never closed as long as the النفس remains in the الجسد, and that no الذنب is أيضاً great for divine المغفرة إذا the التوبة is sincere. Beyond التوبة, ابن القيم argues that the most powerful weapon against الذنب is genuine المحبة of الله. عندما the القلب truly loves الله — experiences His names and attributes as realities, feels connected to Him in الصلاة and الذكر, and finds genuine sweetness in worship — the attractions of الذنب lose their power. The person who is drunk on المحبة of the Creator is not easily seduced by the passing pleasures of disobedience. This is why ابن القيم devotes extensive sections of the الكتاب to cultivating المحبة of الله through reflection on His attributes, his الكرم, and the gifts He has given the المؤمن. The الكتاب أيضاً presents practical weapons against specific الذنوب. For الذنوب of the eyes, it recommends lowering the gaze and filling the القلب with reflection on الآخرة. For الذنوب of the tongue, it recommends silence, الذكر of الله, and التلاوة of القرآن الكريم. For الذنوب of the stomach, it recommends controlling appetite and remembering the accountability on the يوم القيامة. For الذنوب driven by the المحبة of status, it recommends reflection on the insignificance of worldly reputation and the permanence of الله's judgment. Each prescription is tailored to the specific المرض it addresses.
